_readme: Edit any string below, save, then refresh the page in your browser to see the change — no build step needed. Values may contain simple HTML (like <b>, <em>, <span>). Keys match the data-copy="..." attributes in src/shell.html; don't rename keys unless you also update the matching attribute there.

hero.eyebrow: A jazz musician's single practice surface · start shedding now...
hero.h1: Your whole practice session. One tab.
hero.sub:
Mastering your instrument is hard enough — you need your daily practice session to run as smoothly as possible. Stop juggling six PDFs, a metronome app, backing tracks, two YouTube tabs, and a binder full of music.

Just press play and practice.

warmup.headline: This is where every session starts.
warmup.sub: You know the deal: long tones, lip slurs, tonguing, a technical study... and a click-track per exercise that saves your pace and keeps you honest.
warmup.clarke_marking: half=80–120 · Play these exercises legato at first, then very lightly single tongue them

jam.headline: A backing track band that actually sounds good.
jam.sub: Pick a tune, press play. Real changes, real time, real feel — set the tempo and the comping style, then blow over the form.
jam.drummer_hint: groove, and it nudges the comp feel to match

ask.headline: Get early access and a vote on what I build next.
ask.sub: I'm building this in the open. Drop your email for the next release, and tell me what to build on the board. No spam. Just the occasional "here's what's new."
ask.fine_print: ⊘  No spam. Unsubscribe in one click. ~1 email a month.
ask.vote_1_meta: Long tones, lip slurs, flexibility — the whole routine
ask.vote_2_meta: Stack your routine + upload the PDFs you're practicing
ask.vote_3_meta: Streaks, logs, watch your reps add up
ask.vote_4_meta: Tell me what to build — type it in

footer.bio: Built by Chris — Strategist by day, jazz trumpet by night. Woodshedding in public, one tune at a time.
footer.note: Public-domain studies, re-engraved · made for jazz players
